hypothesis: For conformation-dependent molecular design objectives, integrating adaptive diversity constraints derived from 3D conformation-aware representations (e.g., 3D fingerprints or graph-conformer ensembles) into generative optimization will yield higher functional diversity—measured as greater variance in predicted target property and a larger number of unique scaffolds—than using string- or 2D-fingerprint-based constraints, while producing a final mean property score that is not significantly lower (p < 0.05) than that obtained with combined 2D/string constraints.
